title: The strangest way money has ever arrived for you
subtitle: A journal prompt set for finding the evidence you already have that income can arrive unexpectedly.
excerpt: A journal prompt set from Week 5 of the MONEY program. Five prompts for recalling real instances of unexpected income and examining what you did with that evidence.
sourceNotes: Adapted from the Week 5 daily reflection prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you want to counter the belief that unexpected income never happens to you by looking at the actual record.

content.text: A journal prompt set from Week 5 of the MONEY program. Most people, if they look back honestly, have at least one instance of money arriving from an unexpected place. These prompts find it and examine what happened next.
New to journalling as a practice? Read
When did money last arrive in a way I didn't plan?
Write the specific occasion. The amount, the source, the approximate time.
What was my reaction when it arrived?
Write the honest answer. Did you receive it fully? Discount it? Spend it quickly?
What did I tell myself about that unexpected money?
Write the story you gave it. "A fluke." "Lucky timing." "It won't happen again." Or did you let it count?
If I let that example count as real evidence, what does it tell me about how money can arrive?
Write the conclusion you can draw from the evidence rather than the dismissal.
What would I do differently if I believed unexpected income was a pattern, not a fluke?
Write the specific change. Something you would start, stop, or allow.
Adapted from the Week 5 daily reflection prompts in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom and Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
